title: Ophideraan
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Ophideraan.jpg]] |name=Ophideraan |region=*[[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[Southern Core]]The [[:File:MiscellaneousRegions.jpg|Client States and Miscellaneous Regions map]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the portions of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] that fall in grid square K-13 in the [[Southern Core]]. |sector= |system=[[Ophideraan system/Legends|Ophideraan system]] |coordinates=K-13 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons=At least two"[[The Serpent Masters]]" |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class= |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain=*[[Desert/Legends|Desert]] *Rocky canyons *Sinkholes |water= |interest=*[[Great Well]] *[[Slave level]] *[[Cavern of the Serpents]] |flora= |fauna=[[Flying serpent]]s |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] |government=[[Supreme Serpent Master]] |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation=[[Serpent Masters]] }} {{Quote|Oh, I'm clever, Tanith... so clever that I collect vital information for the Rebel Alliance... then get myself stuck in the middle of nowhere so they can't use it!|Luke Skywalker, upon crashing on Ophideraan|}} '''Ophideraan''' was an arid [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] that, by the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], was ruled by a group of bandits known as the [[Serpent Masters]]. Ophideraan's skies were dominated by a unique [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[flying serpent]] that communicated through ultrasonic tones not audible to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] ear—it was through discovery of these tones that the Serpent Masters learned to control and mount these fearsome beasts. The Serpent Masters maintained a society through salvaging wrecks and [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] those unfortunates that crashed onto Ophideraan, using them to maintain the few sources of [[Water/Legends|water]] on the planet. The Serpent Masters' rule was overthrown in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], through the efforts of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agent [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. ==Description== {{Quote|Have I noticed anything about those serpent steeds? Really, Artoo! They're huge, terrifying monsters!|[[C-3PO]], to R2-D2|}} Located in the [[Ophideraan system/Legends|Ophideraan system]] of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]], Ophideraan was an isolated, barren [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with little plant life dotting its surface. However, the world supported a large population of [[flying serpent]]s, which were among the most distinctive life forms native to Ophideraan. The massive winged [[Lizard/Legends|lizards]] ruled Ophideraan's skies, communicating with each other through tones at pitches beyond [[Human/Legends|Human]] hearing. The serpents originated from the [[Great Well]], the main source of [[Water/Legends|water]] on the arid planet, and which was regarded as the source of all life on Ophideraan. Ophideraan had at least a pair of [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. ==History== {{Quote|We crashed on this arid rock long ago, and were taken prisoner by the Serpent Masters.|Josen Shire to Luke Skywalker|}} [[File:Classic_04_25.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Serpent Masters ride their mounts in the skies above Ophideraan.]] By the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Ophideraan was ruled by the [[Serpent Masters]], a group of Human scavengers who had learned how to control and ride upon the planet's native flying serpents. The group's [[Supreme Serpent Master]], a man named [[Tyrann]], not only discovered the arid world but learned the secret of how the flying serpents communicated, creating a device that could replicate their ultrasonic tones and allow for their use as mounts.{{CSWECite|I|283|Flying serpent}} The Serpent Masters made use of a large amount of [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] labor in maintaining their society—among the unfortunates held there was the Human [[Josen Shire]] and his daughter [[Tanith Shire|Tanith]], who had crash-landed on Ophideraan and were immediately taken captive. Tanith was allowed to serve the Serpent Masters offworld by steering wrecked [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to the planet for salvage, and in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] she returned with a friend in tow: [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and budding [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-user. Pressed into servitude by the Serpent Masters, Skywalker immediately began working to overthrow the bandits, starting by attempting to uncover the secret of how they controlled the flying serpents. Skywalker's [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] uncovered how the Serpent Masters were commanding the beasts, and used the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech's]] capabilities to claim a steed of his own. Having learned of the device Tyrann used to maintain his group's dominion over the serpents took the form of a medallion worn around his neck, Skywalker flew his serpent into battle and cut the medallion from Tyrann's neck. Tyrann leapt to his death trying to catch the falling trinket, and the medallion's destruction led to the serpents being turned loose and the downfall of the Serpent Masters. Afterwards, R2-D2 fixed the [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]] and turned it over to the bandits' former slaves, giving them the tools to rule over Ophideraan themselves. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|Who are they, Tanith?"
"Scavengers! They prey off wrecks like this one... and enslave anyone unlucky enough to fall into their hands!|Luke Skywalker and Tanith Shire, upon encountering the Serpent Masters|}} By 0 ABY, Ophideraan was mostly populated by the Serpent Masters and the [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] they enslaved, as they stripped wrecked ships on the planet's surface of all their valuables and survivors. Mostly Human, the Serpent Masters were led by a Supreme Serpent Master, Tyrann, who led their initial settling of the planet. The dozens of slaves trapped on Ophideraan spent their days maintaining the extensive system plumbing and drilling equipment at the heart of the Great Well. ==Locations== {{Quote|What is this place?"
"The Great Well! Source of all life on our planet... birthground of our serpent steeds... and the place you will be judged by our Supreme Master!|Luke Skywalker and [[Varn (individual)|Varn]]|}} The center of life on the planet Ophideraan was its Great Well, the origin point of its flying serpents and the main population center on the arid world. The well was a massive pit that bored deep into the planet's surface, with a large [[Water/Legends|lake]] at the bottom—a large island at the center of the Great Well contained the Serpent Masters' [[slave level]], where their captives spent their days maintaining the Great Well's plumbing and drilling equipment. The Great Well also contained the [[Cavern of the Serpents]], the breeding ground for the beasts. ==Behind the scenes== Ophideraan first appeared as the primary setting of ''[[The Serpent Masters]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic strip]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and released in [[1981]]. Although the planet went unnamed in its first appearance, its name was later given in Tanith Shire's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry.{{DB|character|tanithshire|Shire, Tanith}} ==Appearances== *"[[The Serpent Masters]]" {{Reprint|The Serpent Masters}} {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ophideraan}} *{{CollectorCite|3|ComicScan|ComicScan: Coming Full Circle}} {{Imo}} *{{FactFile|60|TAN 1-4|Tanith Shire}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *{{InsiderCite|88|Comlink}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{DB|character|tanithshire|Shire, Tanith}} {{1stID|Ophideraan}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Ophideraan }} [[Category:Planets in the Colonies]] [[Category:Planets in the Southern Core]] [[Category:Slavery worlds]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]]
